Of Mtn And Mtn For Relief
Off Calendar Mar-24-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF LOREN KAISER's Motion for Relief Under CCP 473(b), to vacate the December 20, 2016, order is granted. Mr. Kaiser has shown excusable neglect warranting relief per CCP 473(b) and doing so comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits. Defendant Bank of America has not explained how it would be prejudiced by the granting of the motion other than the conclusory assertion that there is insufficient time for discovery and trial preparation. The Bank is entitled to have its demurrer to Mr. Kaiser's first amended complaint heard. The court has reviewed the moving papers and Mr. Kaiser's proposed opposition papers on the demurrer and the arguments made in the Bank's opposition papers to this motion relating to the demurrer and now rules on that demurrer: the demurrer is off calendar as to the first cause of action for violation of the CRLA, sustained with ten days leave to amend as to second cause of action for violation of the UCL, and overruled as to the third cause of action for breach of contract. The CRLA cause of action is not alleged against the Bank. The UCL cause of action adequately alleges all required elements of a UCL claim based on the fraud prong except for reliance and Mr. Kaiser is given leave to allege reliance if he can do so in good faith. The fraud cause of action is adequately alleged. Recent case law establishes that the failure to attach a written contract or to allege its terms verbatim is not fatal to a breach of written contract claim. (Miles v. Deutsche Bank National Trust Co. (2015) 236 Cal. App. 4th 394). Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-14-2016 Continued To Nov-09-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgement On The Pleading
Off Calendar Sep-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Adam F. Summerfield of McGuire Woods Law Firm (310-315-8200 ext. 43429) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICAN, N.A.'S Motion For Judgement On The Pleadings is GRANTED without leave to amend as to the claim for violation of the Consumer Legal Remedies Act and with twenty days leave to amend as to the claims for violation of the Unfair Competition Law and breach of contract. Mr. Kaiser's CLRA claims fail because a fraud protection program is a service that is not subject to the CLRA. Mr. Kaiser's UCL and breach of contract claims do not adequately plead the terms of the contract that B of A allegedly breached or some other unfair, unlawful or fraudulent business practice. If he can do so in good faith, Mr. Kaiser is given leave to correct these deficiencies. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)
